name: Enrich company profiles with HubSpot and HTTP / REST API 24/7 for Conversational AI (01662)

parameters.text: =✅ Enrich company profiles with HubSpot and HTTP / REST API 24/7 for Conv — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 8:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Stamp & Shape Request

### Stamp & Shape Request

Shapes the item: writes `requestId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.requestId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Remove Duplicate Requests · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Remove Duplicate Requests

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `requestId`).

Next: Enrich — Primary Provider

### Enrich — Primary Provider

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Primary Hit? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Primary Hit?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.company || $json.name }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Merge Enrichment Paths · ②→ Enrich — Fallback Provider

### Enrich — Fallback Provider

GET https://fallback-enrich.example.com/v1/person — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Merge Enrichment Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Merge Enrichment Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Score Enriched Records

### Score Enriched Records

Custom JavaScript: Score each request 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Upsert into HubSpot

### Upsert into HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Hot Lead? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Hot Lead?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.score >= 80 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Ping Owner Immediately · ②→ Stamp Completion Time

### Ping Owner Immediately

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

# Enrich company profiles with HubSpot and HTTP / REST API 24/7 for Conversational AI (01662)

**Pattern:** Data Enrichment · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Enrich company profiles with HubSpot and HTTP / REST API 24/7 for Conversational AI (01662). It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual conversational ai is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Stamp & Shape Request → Payload Valid? → Remove Duplicate Requests → Enrich — Primary Provider → Primary Hit? → Merge Enrichment Paths → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to data enrichment without manual effort
- Anyone automating conversational ai in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; HubSpot private-app token; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The fallback enrichment provider only fires when the primary genuinely misses (checked explicitly, not assumed) — paying for two enrichment calls per lead when the first one already worked would double the cost for no benefit. Scoring runs after enrichment, not before, so the score reflects the richest data available rather than just what arrived on the original request.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Force the primary provider to miss (e.g. a bad URL) and confirm the fallback picks up the slack.
2. Add a third-provider rung to the waterfall after the fallback.
3. Change the hot-lead threshold and watch how often the immediate-ping branch fires.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your conversational ai process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
